item.title: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 from brain cancer
item.description: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died, his family announced Tuesday.

item.title: How an elementary school custodian earned the title of "Papa Duck"
item.description: Outside of Chicago, at Western Avenue Elementary, the head custodian has been looking out for little ducklings for 29 years. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: New details about Boston-area highway gunman
item.description: The suspect who allegedly fired into a major road near Boston had prior criminal convictions. Jericka Duncan reports on new details about the shooting and the suspect.

item.title: Will Trump seek investment to join China in a "dark factory" future?
item.description: A Chinese manufacturing giant tells CBS News how its sprawling factory runs with a fraction of the human workforce previously required.

item.title: Florida immigration detention center "Alligator Alcatraz" to close, sources say
item.description: The immigration detention center in the Florida Everglades known as "Alligator Alcatraz" is set to close less than a year after it opened, sources told CBS News.

item.title: Plane crashes in Atlantic Ocean, all 11 passengers rescued
item.description: A small plane crashed into the Atlantic Ocean on Tuesday, 50 miles off Vero Beach, Florida, with 11 people on board. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Economic pressure may not push Iranian regime to make peace deal with U.S., expert explains
item.description: President Trump is on his way to Beijing to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before he left, he spoke about the current ceasefire with Iran. Will Todman, a senior fellow in the Middle East Program at CSIS, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Democrats grill Kash Patel on accusations of excessive drinking and other personal behavior
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defended the Pentagon's budget request of $1.5 trillion on Tuesday. At a separate hearing, FBI Director Kash Patel faced questions about his alcohol use and personal behavior.

item.title: California mayor pleads guilty to acting as an illegal agent for China
item.description: In Southern California, the Arcadia mayor has resigned after agreeing to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for China. Specifically, she pled guilty to spreading pro-Communist propaganda in the Asian community there. Anna Schecter has more.

item.title: Trump on "the only thing that matters" in dealing with Iran
item.description: President Trump said Americans' financial situation isn't motivating him to make a deal, "Not even a little bit," and that he is only focused on preventing Iran from getting nuclear weapons.

item.title: What's Trump's game plan in China amid Iran war?
item.description: President Trump headed to China on Tuesday for a high-stakes and highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before leaving, Mr. Trump downplayed the need for Xi to intervene in the stalled peace talks with Iran. Anna Coren reports from Beijing.

item.title: CPI surged in April as inflation soars to highest level in almost 3 years
item.description: U.S. consumer prices rose in April, fueled by a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Trump says he is not backing down as war with Iran spikes Americans' cost of living
item.description: As the war with Iran drags on, the cost of living is rising faster than wages, putting a major squeeze on middle and working-class Americans. Kelly O'Grady reports.

item.title: Trump administration to name David Venturella as interim ICE chief
item.description: The Trump administration plans to name longtime immigration official David Venturella as the interim head of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, replacing acting director Todd Lyons, a spokesperson and two U.S. officials said.

item.title: Iran says it's ready to "teach a lesson" if U.S. launches new attacks
item.description: Iran warns any new U.S. attacks will bring a "bad result," as President Trump acknowledges the ceasefire is faltering and violence flares in Lebanon.

item.title: Marty Makary resigns as FDA commissioner after internal tensions
item.description: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary resigned his position, stepping aside amid a swirl of reports that his tenure was coming to an end.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: LBJ calls for equal voting rights after Selma march in 1965
item.description: Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio coverage of President Lyndon B. Johnson's 1965 speech to Congress, calling for equal voting rights for Black Americans.

item.title: How Pete Hegseth is justifying the $1.5 trillion military funding request
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill grilled Pentagon officials on Tuesday over the military's $1.5 trillion funding request for the 2027 fiscal year. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.

item.title: Health officials monitoring Americans from hantavirus cruise outbreak
item.description: International health officials say the number of confirmed or suspected hantavirus cases stemming from the cruise ship outbreak has climbed to 11, including the three people who died. CBS News' Ian Lee and Dr. Céline Gounder have more.

item.title: Patel denies alleged excessive drinking in Senate testimony
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel again pushed back against allegations of excessive drinking at a Senate hearing on Tuesday. Mario Parker, managing editor at Bloomberg, and Paul Kane, congressional reporter for NOTUS, join to discuss.

item.title: White House invites U.S. business titans to Trump's China summit
item.description: The White House invited 16 top U.S. executives to join President Trump on his trip to China. CBS News contributor Patrick McGee joins "The Takeout" to discuss what the move could signal to Beijing.

item.title: Americans feel financial pinch of Iran war
item.description: The new inflation report shows that the war with Iran is taking a toll on Americans' finances, but President Trump said on Tuesday that's not a priority when making decisions about the conflict. CBS News' Javier David and Olivia Rinaldi have more.

item.title: Latest details on Musk-OpenAI court battle as Sam Altman takes the stand
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman took the stand on Tuesday to defend himself against a lawsuit brought by his former business partner, Elon Musk. Paresh Dave, senior writer for Wired, joins to unpack the case so far.

item.title: Brandon Clarke of the Memphis Grizzlies dies at age 29
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has died at the age of 29, the team announced.

item.title: Where the hantavirus cruise ship outbreak stands as health officials monitor 18 Americans
item.description: The total number of confirmed and suspected hantavirus cases from a cruise ship outbreak has reached 11. Health officials in Nebraska and Georgia are monitoring the 18 Americans who may have been exposed. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has the latest.

item.title: What to know about today's primaries in Nebraska and West Virginia
item.description: Voters are going to the polls in Nebraska and West Virginia on Tuesday, with Democrats vying for the chance to run in an open seat in Nebraska that the party has long been eyeing.

item.title: Social Security's cost-of-living adjustment could reach nearly 4% in 2027
item.description: A larger COLA would boost monthly checks for retirees, but also strain Social Security's already depleted trust funds.

item.title: Canvas' parent company strikes deal with hackers to delete stolen data
item.description: A hacking group named ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for the Canvas breach and threatened to leak data involving 275 million individuals if schools did not pay a ransom.

item.title: Extended interview: Lizzo
item.description: Lizzo sits down with "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King to discuss her third album, "Bitch," and how it reflects her current state in life, her ongoing legal battle, weight loss journey, embracing her body and more.

item.title: Christopher Nolan: Don't approach my films like a "crossword puzzle"
item.description: While his most recent work was put under a fact-checking microscope, director believes his films are best enjoyed simply as amusement
